title: Request for records concerning Peregrine Technologies, Nightingale Security DFR program, and BRINC Responder UAS integration

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for records concerning Peregrine Technologies, Nightingale Security DFR program, and BRINC Responder UAS integration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request electronic copies of all records, communications, and attachments from January 1, 2018 to present concerning the Peregrine Technologies data-integration / analytics platform and its integration with other surveillance or first-responder systems used by the San Pablo Police Department (SPPD).

1. Contracts & Procurements
All contracts, amendments, renewals, purchase orders, invoices, or memoranda between the City / SPPD and Peregrine Technologies, including those referenced in Resolution 2021-060 and the Dec 2023 Year-End Brochure listing a $100,000 license (12/18/22 – 12/17/23).
All contracts, amendments, or staff reports for Resolution 2023-110 (three-year Nightingale Security DFR agreement, $644,311) and File #24-410 (BRINC Responder Operations Bundle purchase).
Any Statements of Work, proposals, or quotes listing Peregrine modules or features such as video analytics, real-time insights, resource-allocation tools, dashboards, or heat-mapping.

2. Technical Integrations & System Design
Documents describing interfaces, APIs, or data feeds connecting Peregrine with: ALPR systems; ShotSpotter / SoundThinking; CCTV / situational cameras; body-worn camera systems; CAD / RMS databases; and Nightingale or BRINC drone video feeds.
Configuration manuals or network diagrams showing where data flows between these systems.

3. Analytics Logic & “Insights”
Any documentation, policy, or training material defining how Peregrine produces “insights,” “video analytics,” or “real-time decision” outputs.
Records describing rules, thresholds, weighting, or triggers used for alerts, heat maps, or resource-placement recommendations.
User guides or presentations provided to SPPD officers or analysts explaining how to interpret or act on Peregrine insights.

4. Bias Testing / Auditing / Impact Assessments
Reports, audits, or internal reviews evaluating accuracy, false-positive rates, or disparate impact of Peregrine analytics, ALPR hits, or DFR dispatches.
Data showing alert, incident, or DFR-launch counts by neighborhood or census tract (from 2019 to present).
Correspondence with Peregrine, Nightingale, or BRINC discussing equity, bias, fairness, or community impact.

5. Data Retention & Sharing
Current and prior data-retention schedules for Peregrine-related data and any data-sharing agreements with vendors or partner agencies.
Clauses or memos describing vendor access rights to SPPD data for analytics, testing, or model training.

6. Public Relations & Performance Claims
Talking points, press releases, or communications referencing Peregrine’s role in crime reduction (e.g., claims of an “80 percent crime reduction” or use of “innovative AI tools,” as reported in Police1 articles dated Dec 18 2024 and Apr 10 2025).
Any presentations, vendor demos, or community-outreach slides referencing “AI,” “video analytics,” “insights,” or “real-time decision making.”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Heather Gilligan

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: Employee Communications Records - Wallingford Public Schools

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Employee Communications Records - Wallingford Public Schools
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All electronic communications (emails, text messages, instant messages, social media communications) sent or received by Lisette Giacobbe during her employment from January 1, 2024 to present
- All communications between school district employees containing the names "Conrad," "John Conrad," "Emelia Conrad," or "Sage Conrad" from January 1, 2024 to present
- Complete personnel file for Lisette Giacobbe including any disciplinary actions, complaints, performance evaluations, or incident reports

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all email metadata (sender, recipient, subject line, timestamp, etc.) sent or received by the following addresses: jshaw@psc.ga.gov, fjohnson@psc.ga.gov, techols@psc.ga.gov, lmcdonald@psc.ga.gov, tpridemore@psc.ga.gov, and gapsc@psc.ga.gov. The date range for the search should span 12/01/2025 to the date of receipt of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

PT

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Butte County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Butte County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The Colorado Springs Police Department (“CSPD”) has received your records request dated 2/1/2026, and it is under review. The records you have requested are considered criminal justice records as defined in Colorado Revised Statute § 24-72-302(4) which are held in the sole possession of CSPD, a law enforcement agency.  The Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act (“CCJRA”) as codified in C.R.S. § 24-72-301 et. seq. governs the release of criminal justice records.  The time response deadlines contained within CORA do not apply to requests for criminal justice records governed by the CCJRA.

Kind Regards,

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Cruz County Elections Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Cruz County Elections Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting from your county, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: Any emails containing the words "60 Minutes"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Any emails containing the words "60 Minutes"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any emails that contain the words "60 Minutes" sent by the following list of people:

- Commissioner Brendan Carr
- Chief of Staff Greg Watson
- Legal Advisor  Danielle Thumann
- Legal Advisor Arpan A. Sura
- Assistant Drema Johnson

The records can be retrieved by a keyword search email subjects, recipients and message bodies from Jan. 1, 2025 to the date of the search

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ben Welsh

title: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie Frederick
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026

Elise Miller White, County Clerk
100 South Main Street, P.O. Box 1167
Franklin, WV 26807

Dear, Elise Miller White
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Pendleton County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.
Sincerely, Kylie Frederick
kyliejfrederick04@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Ms. Frederick,

I have reviewed your request and am working on gathering the requested documents. I anticipate having everything by Friday, March 20, 2026.

Thank you.

Elise

communications.communication:
Kylie,

 

I have attached the information you requested regarding the Opioid funds received and spent. We do not use an application form for requests to received funds. Generally the organization will either request by mail or email to the County Administrator or one of our County Commissioners. I have attached a copy of our Revenue and Expenditure ledger showing funds we have received and disbursed. I have also attached copies of the minutes (relevant section highlighted) where the County Commission considered the requests for opioid funds. 

The procedure is as follows: a request is made for the funds, the Commission considers the request and either approves or disapproves the request. That information will be noted in the minutes. Our County Commissioners are Carl Hevener, President, Roger Dahmer and Jimmie Bennett.

If you require additional information or clarification on any of the above, please let me know.

Thank you.

title: Johnny Novielo Death Records (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Johnny Novielo Death Records (Immigration and Customs Enforcement)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records pertaining to the detention, care, death, and post-death inquiry for Johnny Noviello, (DOB: 7/1/1975; DOD: 6/25/2025), who died in ICE custody after incarceration at BOP's Federal Detention Center - Miami facility. See https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/reports/ddr_NOVIELLOJohnny.pdf. 

This request includes, but is not limited to, the following records: 

1. Any Mortality Review  and associated working papers conducted by ICE Health Services Corps or the Bureau of Prisons
2. Any Death Review conducted by ICE ERAU or BOP
3. Any Healthcare and Security Compliance Analysis or similar document created by Creative Corrections or another Contractor
4. All extant video showing Mr. Noviello in ICE or BOP custody.
5. All BOP and/or ICE medical records for Mr. Noviello. 
6. Any Executive Summary or Findings information regarding Mr. Noviello's death
7. Any autopsy report for Mr. Noviello, complete with toxicology. 
8. Records of any contract discrepancy reports or similar findings.

I request expedited processing of this request pursuant to 6 C.F.R. 5.5(e) and declare under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding: 

1. I am a member of the news media investigating and reporting edited pieces on deaths in ICE custody and abuses of power by federal law enforcement agencies. See, e.g., https://theappeal.org/authors/andrew-free/. 
2. Mr. Noviello is the first ICE detainee to die in a BOP facility in over a decade. 
3. His death in BOP's FDC Miami following his imprisonment at Krome Service Processing Center has been the subject of widespread media coverage about which serious questions exist as to the care the government provided him. 
4. ICE's Miami Field Office has overseen the custody and care of at least six (6) people, including Mr. Noviello, who died in ICE custody in FY25--the most of any field office in the United States.
5. An urgent need exists for the public to obtain, review, and understand the circumstances surrounding Mr. Noviello's death in custody as ICE deaths continue to rise to their highest levels since the Covid pandemic and the agency is poised to massively expand ICE detention capacity.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2025-ICFO-53004
communications.communication:
08/07/2025 

Andrew  Free 
MuckRock News DEPT MR 190512 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2025-ICFO-53004 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 8/4/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to detainee death Johnny Noviello.  Your request was received in this office on 8/4/2025. 

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you’re able to narrow the scope of your request please contact our office. Narrowing the scope may speed up the search process. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. 

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. 

We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 

If you have any questions, please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at 500 12th Street, SW, Washington DC 20536 or (866) 633-1182. Additionally, you have a right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation.  If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974.  You may contact OGIS as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 

Your request has been assigned reference number 2025-ICFO-53004. Please use this number in future correspondence. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2025-ICFO-53004
communications.communication:
I appeal. ICE’s invocation of a blanket 7A exception is improper. As an initial matter, there is no conceivable “enforcement proceeding” connected to any of these records or their collection or production. Consequently, there is nothing release of the requested records could interfere with. ICE’s death investigations are neither criminal nor civil in nature and there is no administrative proceeding at their conclusion. They are not conducted by law enforcement personnel, and no consequences attach when contract staff lie or destroy evidence, or when witnesses are spirited away without questioning.  ICE asserts the power nationwide to conduct death reviews free from the pesky facts and accountability that plague true medicolegal death investigations.  

Even if the agency were, for the sake of argument, conducting an investigation that could trigger exemption 7A, the blanket assertion of the exemption to withhold everything — even records and portions of records that have already been publicly released, violates the law.

communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2026-ICAP-00253, 2025-ICFO-53004
communications.communication:
03/02/2026 

Andrew  Free 
MuckRock News DEPT MR 190512 
Boston, Massachusetts 02115 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2026-ICAP-00253, 2025-ICFO-53004 

Dear Requester: 

The Department of Homeland Security has received your letter appealing the adverse determination of your Freedom of Information Act/Privacy Act (FOIA/PA) request by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Your appeal, dated on 2/5/2026, was received on 2/26/2026. 

The Government Information Law Division acknowledges your appeal request of and is assigning it number 2026-ICAP-00253 for tracking purposes.  Please reference this number in any future communications about your appeal. 

A high number of FOIA/PA requests have been received by the Department.  Accordingly, we have adopted the court-sanctioned practice of generally handling backlogged appeals on a first-in, first-out basis.   While we will make every effort to process your appeal on a timely basis, there may be some delay in resolving this matter.  Should you have any questions concerning the processing of your appeal, please contact the ICE FOIA Office/Public Liaison at ICEFOIAPublicLiaison@ice.dhs.gov or (866) 633-1182. 

Sincerely, 
 
Sara Jazayeri 
Chief 
Government Information Law Division 
ICE Office of the Principal Legal Advisor 
U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with William S. Hein & Co. relating to the licensing of the “HeinOnline” database;
2) All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; and
3) All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with William S. Hein & Co. for the “HeinOnline” database. 

Please do not limit your search to strictly direct agreements or renewals between the institution and William S. Hein & Co., but include any agreements and related documentation through consortia such as the Hawai’i Library Consortium, the Greater Western Library Alliance (GWLA), or Lyrasis.

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of the “HeinOnline” database.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ellen Dubinsky

communications.communication:
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of 3/23/2026, Reference # W036426-032326 
Dear Joey , 
The City of Redmond received your public records request on 3/23/2026, regarding “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Provide a copy of the signed award agreement between the City of Redmond and the Washington Auto Theft Prevention Authority that helped fund Flock ALRPs for the city. The city council has mentioned this several times while discussing the removal of Flock ALRPs from the city. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Joey JJ Jr S. 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2248377/” 
Your request will be forwarded out to the applicable department(s) for response. 
You will be notified regarding the status of your request by 3/31/2026 (5 business days per RCW 42.56). 
Thank you for your submission, 
City of Redmond 
Public Records Coordinator

communications.subject: Public Record Request :: W036426-032326
communications.communication:
--- City of Redmond --- 
 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of 3/23/2026, Reference # W036426-032326 
Dear Joey , 
The City of Redmond received your public records request on 3/23/2026, regarding “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Provide a copy of the signed award agreement between the City of Redmond and the Washington Auto Theft Prevention Authority that helped fund Flock ALRPs for the city. The city council has mentioned this several times while discussing the removal of Flock ALRPs from the city. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Joey JJ Jr S. 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2248377/”. 
The City has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Please log on to your account on the Public Records Request portal and click on the "view files" button to retrieve your documents. All responsive records have been produced. This request is now closed. If you have any follow-up questions regarding the closing of this request, please promptly contact us by responding to this message. If you would like a review of your request, the process can be found in Section 10 of Redmond's Public Records Disclosure Rules of Procedure. If you are seeking further records not covered in this request, please submit a new request through our  This request is closed and the City is no longer fulfilling it. Per state law (RCW 42.56.550), the Public Records Act's one-year statute of limitations starts on this date. The statute of limitations defines the maximum time limit to file a lawsuit if you determine that your records request was not fulfilled appropriately. Thank you for contacting the City of Redmond. 

Cordially, 

Sara Frasier-Jackson City of Redmond Public Records Coordinator

title: pfau morrissey communications

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: pfau morrissey communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All communications between James Morrissey and the respondants in Pfau v. Foster & Garbus (Index no. 2009-8236) following the entry of the May 2010 consent decree.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Mellins

communications.communication:
November 7, 2024 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000742-103024 Dear Mr. Mellins: Pursuant to Public Officers Law §89(3)(a), the Office of the Attorney General acknowledges receipt of the above-referenced FOIL request. We are performing a diligent search for the records you request.  We will notify you of the status of your request on or before December 9, 2024. If we should locate any documents that respond to your request, and if we provide them in electronic format, we will notify you of any fee in advance.  If we provide paper copies, we will also notify you of any fee in advance; for paper copies, the first five pages would be provided free of charge, and the cost for the remaining pages would be $.25 per page. Sincerely, Abisola Fatade Assistant Attorney General 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests.

December 9, 2024 
RE: Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000742-103024 
Dear Mr. Mellins: 
Please be advised that we are diligently engaged in the process of preparing a response to your above-referenced FOIL request. We estimate that, by February 6, 2025, we will be able to begin producing to you the records that respond to your request. This additional time is needed for the following reasons: • the age of the records that may respond to your request; • the number of locations that must be searched in order to respond to your request; and • the detailed nature of the review required to respond to your request. We will contact you on or before the date mentioned. Thank you for your cooperation in this matter. Sincerely, Abisola Fatade Assistant Attorney General

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000742-103024
communications.communication:
March 25, 2026 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000742-103024 Dear Sam Mellins: This email is in response to the above-­referenced FOIL request. The Office of the Attorney General has conducted a diligent search for the records that you have requested. Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request.  Please be advised that some of the records that respond to your request have been withheld in their entirety, or portions have been redacted and appear as blacked out in the documents produced to you.  These records, or portions of records, are exempt from disclosure pursuant to: • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a), which provides that records that are exempted from disclosure by state or federal statute are exempt from disclosure under FOIL.  The records constitute attorney work product, which is exempt from disclosure under Civil Practice Law and Rules § 3101(c); and • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. This is our eighth and final response to the above-referenced request. Accordingly, this FOIL request is now closed. Sincerely, Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests. 
 View this request in the My Request Center.

title: Records Related to Jeffrey Epstein, Client Lists, and Known Associates

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Related to Jeffrey Epstein, Client Lists, and Known Associates
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records, reports, memoranda, notes, emails, interview transcripts, investigative files, and any other materials in the possession of the FBI that reference or relate to:

Jeffrey Epstein’s associates, co-conspirators, or known affiliates

Any "client list" or documented individuals connected to Epstein through criminal investigation, surveillance, flight logs, or seized evidence

Any materials identifying individuals who were investigated in connection with Jeffrey Epstein or his known associates

Any FBI files related to the prosecution, investigation, or handling of Jeffrey Epstein’s activities, including documents after his death

Any materials referencing Ghislaine Maxwell’s trial that pertain to sealed documents or unnamed parties.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tara Hill

To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby
request the following records: All records, reports, memoranda, notes, emails,
interview transcripts, investigative files, and any other materials in the
possession of the FBI that reference or relate to: Jeffrey Epsteinâ€™s
associates, co-conspirators, or known affiliates Any "client list" or
documented individuals connected to Epstein through criminal investigation,
surveillance, flight logs, or seized evidence Any materials identifying
individuals who were investigated in connection with Jeffrey Epstein or his
known associates Any FBI files related to the prosecution, investigation, or
handling of Jeffrey Epsteinâ€™s activities, including documents after his death
Any materials referencing Ghislaine Maxwellâ€™s trial that pertain to sealed
documents or unnamed parties. The requested documents will be made available
to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial
purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would
inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would
prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this
matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20
business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Tara Hill Upload documents
directly:
https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Ffederal-
bureau-of-investigation-10%252Frecords-related-to-jeffrey-epstein-client-
lists-and-known-
associates-189781%252F%253Femail%253Defoia%252540subscriptions.fbi.gov&url_auth_token=AAAj2HpE8pW7NuC8iPrfw4mvFg0%3A1ucb2w%3ADJtYyBo8_nSkuSL6YlYtnOrnzHW3O7eNNUX2eTXcrZc

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBIâ€™s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBIâ€™s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the â€˜Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request toolâ€™ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: NYC DOE| Hillcrest High School discplinary records

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NYC DOE| Hillcrest High School discplinary records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) All records and writing (including all investigative materials) regarding any allegation or complaint of misconduct (in any form) brought against any Hillcrest High School employee or volunteer from 6/28/2014 to the date this request is received.
2) All settlement and separation agreements, including resignations "in lieu of termination" or "following allegations of misconduct," of any Hillcrest High School employee or volunteer from 6/28/2014 to the date this request is received.

3)We're also requesting a FOIA log for related requests to misconduct at Hillcrest High School from 6/28/2014. Please include:

- The log or file number
- The date the request was received
- The name of the persons/organization (besides ourselves) making requests related to the above
- A description of the information sought
- The date the response was sent
- The type of response your office sent (granted, denied or partial release)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If you determine that some but not all the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, I ask that you redact it for the time being and make the rest available on a rolling basis. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge and processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I also ask that our request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available (email: holler@bwd.nyc) – if not, secure file transfer, thumb drive, or CD-ROM. I am also available via phone at 718.360.6359 to discuss any questions you have about the request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bradford William Davis

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NYC DOE| Hillcrest High School discplinary records

title: pfau reports (Office of the Attorney General - New York)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: pfau reports (Office of the Attorney General - New York)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All reports filed pursuant to the consent decree/settlement in Pfau v. Foster & Garbus (Index no. 2009-8236) detailing:
1. The number of judgments vacated and the amounts of such judgments
2. The number of defendants re-served
3. The number of defendants who received a return of funds paid on default judgments and the amounts thereof.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Mellins

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000744-103124
communications.communication: November 8, 2024 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000744-103124 Dear Mr. Mellins: Pursuant to Public Officers Law §89(3)(a), the Office of the Attorney General acknowledges receipt of the above-referenced FOIL request. We are performing a diligent search for the records you request.  We will notify you of the status of your request on or before December 10, 2024. If we should locate any documents that respond to your request, and if we provide them in electronic format, we will notify you of any fee in advance.  If we provide paper copies, we will also notify you of any fee in advance; for paper copies, the first five pages would be provided free of charge, and the cost for the remaining pages would be $.25 per page. Sincerely, Abisola Fatade Assistant Attorney General

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000744-103124
communications.communication:
December 10, 2024 
RE: Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000744-103124 
Dear Mr. Mellins: 
Please be advised that we are diligently engaged in the process of preparing a response to your above-referenced FOIL request. We estimate that, by February 10, 2025, we will be able to begin producing to you the records that respond to your request. This additional time is needed for the following reasons: 
• limited resources available at the agency; 
• the number of locations that must be searched in order to respond to your request; and 
• the detailed nature of the review required to respond to your request. 
We will contact you on or before the date mentioned. 
Thank you for your cooperation in this matter. 
Sincerely, 
Abisola Fatade 
Assistant Attorney General 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests. 
 View this request in the My Request Center.

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000744-103124
communications.communication:
February 11, 2025 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000744-103124 Dear Mr. Mellins: This email is in response to the above-referenced FOIL request. The Office of the Attorney General is conducting a diligent search for the records that you have requested. Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request.  Please be advised that this is our initial response to the above-referenced request.  We intend to continue producing records to you on a rolling basis until we have provided all additional, responsive, and non-exempt records and issued our final determination of your FOIL request.  We estimate that, by March 19, 2025, we will be able to produce to you the next installment of responsive records. Sincerely, Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests.

March 19, 2025 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000744-103124 Dear Mr. Mellins: This email is in response to the above-referenced FOIL request. The Office of the Attorney General has conducted a diligent search for the records that you have requested. Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request.  Please be advised that this is our second response to the above-referenced request.  We intend to continue producing records to you on a rolling basis until we have provided all additional, responsive, and non-exempt records and issued our final determination of your FOIL request.  We estimate that, by May 30, 2025, we will be able to produce to you the next installment of responsive records. Sincerely, Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests.

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000744-103124
communications.communication:
March 25, 2026 
RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000744-103124 
Dear Sam Mellins: 
This email is in response to the above-referenced FOIL request. 

Please be advised that the Office of the Attorney General has completed its diligent search and has located no additional records that respond to your request. 

Please be advised that portions of the records previously provided to you are exempt from disclosure and were redacted pursuant to: 

• Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. 

You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. 
This FOIL request is now closed. 

Sincerely, Records Access Officer

title: New & Currently Active Law Enforcement Personnel Investigations (Washington State Criminal Justice Training Commission)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: New & Currently Active Law Enforcement Personnel Investigations (Washington State Criminal Justice Training Commission)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents regarding citizen complaints and Internal Affairs investigations resulting in disciplinary actions taken in reference to law enforcement personnel conducting themselves in an unprofessional manner whether it be to the member of the community or internally within their department/jurisdiction. Specifically these following listed individuals : 

Lionel Carmona - WA Gambling Commission
Cole Hecker - detective - Grandview/Zillah
Trevor Herrud - WSP
Jared Hunt - Yakima Sheriff Deputy
Garrett Stephens - Yakima Sheriff Deputy
Wes Rasmussen - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Kyle Cameron - Yakima Sheriff Deputy
Jesse Rojas - Yakima Sheriff Sgt
Jesus Silva - Yakima Sheriff
Christian Sandoval - Yakima Sheriff
James Sandall - Yakima Sheriff

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Candace B Cupples

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R002625-010526
communications.communication:
Dear Candace Cupples: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of WSCJTC. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/5/2026 and given the reference number R002625-010526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Documents regarding citizen complaints and Internal Affairs investigations resulting in disciplinary actions taken in reference to law enforcement personnel conducting themselves in an unprofessional manner whether it be to the member of the community or internally within their department/jurisdiction. Specifically these following listed individuals : 

Lionel Carmona - WA Gambling Commission 
Cole Hecker - detective - Grandview/Zillah 
Trevor Herrud - WSP 
Jared Hunt - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Garrett Stephens - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Wes Rasmussen - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Kyle Cameron - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Jesse Rojas - Yakima Sheriff Sgt 
Jesus Silva - Yakima Sheriff 
Christian Sandoval - Yakima Sheriff 
James Sandall - Yakima Sheriff 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Candace B Cupples 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2170524/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted within 5 business days with the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
 
Be aware that if you do not download your provided records or installment of records within 30 days after the WSCJTC provided them, the WSCJTC will inform you about the abandonment of your request and close your record request per WAC 139-02-090(10). 
WSCJTC

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of January 05, 2026, Reference # R002625-010526 
Dear Candace, 
The WSCJTC received a public records request from you on January 05, 2026. You requested the following: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Documents regarding citizen complaints and Internal Affairs investigations resulting in disciplinary actions taken in reference to law enforcement personnel conducting themselves in an unprofessional manner whether it be to the member of the community or internally within their department/jurisdiction. Specifically these following listed individuals : 

Lionel Carmona - WA Gambling Commission 
Cole Hecker - detective - Grandview/Zillah 
Trevor Herrud - WSP 
Jared Hunt - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Garrett Stephens - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Wes Rasmussen - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Kyle Cameron - Yakima Sheriff Deputy

Jesus Silva - Yakima Sheriff 
Christian Sandoval - Yakima Sheriff 
James Sandall - Yakima Sheriff 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Candace B Cupples 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2170524/" 
The first installment of records responsive to your request is available to you now in the WSCJTC Records Center. Click the following link  to login to the Public Records Center and retrieve the records. Public Records Request - R002625-010526 
We estimate the next installment to this request will be available on or before 4/2/2026.  If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly.  Conversely, if the agency encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you promptly with a revised estimated date. As staff is processing the request, a copying fee estimate may be provided if your requests from the past calendar year exceeds 250 electronic pages or 100 printed pages, as provided in WAC 139-02-070(10). 
Please be aware that if you do not download your provided installment of records within 30 days after today's date, the WSCJTC will inform you about the abandonment of your request and close your record request. 
Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. If you have any questions, please contact us through the Public Record Center. 
Sincerely, 
Tabitha Stewart 
Forms and Records Analyst 
Certification

--- Please respond above this line --- 

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of January 05, 2026, Reference # R002625-010526. 
Dear Candace Cupples, 
The WSCJTC received a public information request from you on January 05, 2026. Your request mentioned: 
“To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Documents regarding citizen complaints and Internal Affairs investigations resulting in disciplinary actions taken in reference to law enforcement personnel conducting themselves in an unprofessional manner whether it be to the member of the community or internally within their department/jurisdiction. Specifically these following listed individuals : 

Lionel Carmona - WA Gambling Commission 
Cole Hecker - detective - Grandview/Zillah 
Trevor Herrud - WSP 
Jared Hunt - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Garrett Stephens - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Wes Rasmussen - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Kyle Cameron - Yakima Sheriff Deputy 
Jesse Rojas - Yakima Sheriff Sgt 
Jesus Silva - Yakima Sheriff 
Christian Sandoval - Yakima Sheriff 
James Sandall - Yakima Sheriff 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Candace B Cupples 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2170524/” 
The WSCJTC has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request.  Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve the appropriate responsive documents. 
No information has been redacted or withheld from the enclosed records. We do not upload records to portals. You may download these records and distribute them how you wish. Public Records Request - R002625-010526 
This completes your request. Your request is now closed. Per Cousins v. Dept. of Corrections (2024), I am required to inform you this is the WSCJTC's final response to this public records request. The WSCJTC does not intend to further address this request. The PRA’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of the date of this communication. 
If you have any questions, please contact me within 30 days by responding to this email, or by letter at 19010 1st Avenue South Burien, Washington 98148. Thank you for your request. 
Sincerely, 
Tabitha Stewart 
Forms and Records Analyst 
Certification

title: Gerald Selman settlement

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Gerald Selman settlement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any settlement between the City of Detroit and Gerald Selman / GDL Holdings LLC that came from case no. 22-009573-CH in Michigan's 3rd Circuit Court.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Fw: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Gerald Selman settlement
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on March 3, 2026. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, March 4, 2026.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before March 25, 2026.

Your request is being handled by Amanda Kelly. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Amanda Kelly at kellya@detroitmi.gov<mailto:kellya@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Gerald Selman settlement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following Michigan Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on March 3, 2026. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on March 25, 2026. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request No. 2601313, Dated March 3, 2026, Concerning City of Detroit Records Pertaining to Case No. 22-009573-CH
communications.communication:
Please see the attached response to the above-referenced FOIA request.


Amanda Kelly
Senior Assistant Corporation Counsel
City of Detroit-Law Dept.
Freedom of Information Act Section (FOIA)
Coleman A. Young Municipal Center
2 Woodward Avenue, Suite 500
Detroit, Michigan 48226
Email: kellya@detroitmi.gov<mailto:kellya@detroitmi.gov>

WARNING: This communication may include, in whole or in part, information protected by attorney-client privilege, the attorney work product doctrine, the deliberative process privilege, or by other privilege. This communication is intended solely for receipt by the individual or entity to whom it was addressed. If you are not the intended recipient, any use of the communication is neither allowed nor intended. If you received this E-mail in error, please securely delete.

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Murray State University)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Murray State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

communications.subject: Open Records Response - Greg Chick
communications.communication:
Mr. Chick:

Please find attached Murray State University's response to your Open
Records Request dated March 23, 2026. Thank you.

Jill

Jill Hunt
Senior Executive Coordinator for the President and
  Coordinator for Board Relations
Murray State University
Room 218, Wells Hall
Murray, KY  42071
(270) 809-3763

title: UAS Deployments 2025

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: UAS Deployments 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Police Department Records :: D000484-012526
communications.communication:
Dear Rose  Terse, 
Thank you for your interest in public records from the Everett Police Department.  Your request has been received and is being processed.  Your request was received in this office on January 25, 2026 and given the reference number D000484-012526 for tracking purposes. 
 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199229/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant record specialists to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Everett Public Records Center.

communications.communication:

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of January 25, 2026, Reference # D000484-012526 
Dear Rose  Terse, 
The Everett Police Department received a public records request from you on January 25, 2026. You requested the following: 
"To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199229/" 
Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection and/or copying. The undersigned will be coordinating the collection, review and production of responsive records. 
We reasonably believe that public records (if any) responsive to this request will be available on or before 3/2/2026.  If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly.  Conversely, if the Everett Police Department encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you promptly with a revised estimated date. 
Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. 
Sincerely, 
Brent Flagg 
Public Disclosure Manager 
Everett Police Department 
425-257-8471

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2199229/" 
In response to your request, the Everett Police Department has compiled the following records responsive to your request: 
2025 Flight reports for the drone as a first responder flights and log of other drone flights. 
The cost for these records is as follows: 
$0.05 per each 4 files (436 files/4=109)  109x $0.05= $5.45 
Total: $5.45 
Per your request, we are providing the attached invoice before release of the records. Please see the attached invoice for payment information.  Once payment is received, EPD will provide the records. 
If the requestor or a representative of the requestor fails to claim, inspect or review the records within thirty-days of this notice or make arrangements to do so, the City will deem the request as abandoned and closed, pursuant to RCW 44-14-040. Additionally, if the City has issued an invoice and payment(s) is not made within thirty-days of this notice, the City will deem the request as abandoned and closed. 
Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. 
Sincerely, 
Brent Flagg 
Public Disclosure Manager 
Everett Police Department 
425-257-8471 As this is the Everett Police Department’s final and definite response, RCW 42.56.550(6) one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started with this notice. If you should have any questions related to this request, please let us know within ten days.

title: Auror (Lynnwood Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Auror (Lynnwood Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All emails and email notifications sent or received by agency personnel that include the domain "@auror.co". This includes but is not limited to notifications of incident reports, evidence exports, event updates, and other communications generated by the Auror software.
2. All exported reports generated by the Auror software. This includes reports exported in any format (PDF, HTML, JSON, etc.) and covers all types of reports such as evidence reports, incident reports.
3. All incident of investigative reports mentioning or involving the Auror software. This includes any reports or records where the Auror software was used to document, track, or investigate incidents.
4.  SOW, contracts, MOU, user manuals or technical documentation regarding the Auror platform. 

To assist with locating these records, the Auror software is a platform used for tracking, monitoring, and managing various incidents and retail crime investigations. It includes functionalities for exporting reports and sending email notifications.

Responsive documents should be from 1/1/2020 until the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse
