communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Lynnwood, WA public records request #25-1758
communications.communication:
City of Lynnwood, WA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-1758: RE: Public Records Clarification Dear Requestor, The City of Lynnwood Police Department has received a public records request from you. This letter is to clarify what information you are seeking from the Police Department in order to identify the responsive records. Please clarify the following: "All incident of investigative reports mentioning or involving the Auror software. This includes any reports or records where the Auror software was used to document, track, or investigate incidents." -Please provide clarification for number 3. Are you just wanting cases that we made arrests on using the software and or mentioned in a case? If so this is something we wouldnt be able to do as we cannot search key words in case narratives only emails. If I have misinterpreted your intended request please let me know. We look forward to receiving your clarification at your earliest opportunity so that work on your request can continue. Please note that if Police Records does not receive clarification within a month of this message, we will deliver records that we interpret are responsive to your request and will consider the request closed. Should you have any questions, please feel free to respond to this email through your portal account, or contact Police Records at (425) 670-5600. Please note that staff do not routinely monitor closed requests. Respectfully, Lynnwood Police Records ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Lynnwood, WA.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.communication:
City of Lynnwood, WA ************************************************************************ Record request #25-1758 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Requestor, The City of Lynnwood is writing in response to your request for Public Records. Please note that it has been 30 calendar days since your (clarification) was sent you to. We have not received (a response) and now consider the request closed.  At this time the Lynnwood Police Department has closed this request. There is a one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review under the Public Records Act, which has started to run because the Department does not intend to further address this request. Please note that staff do not routinely monitor closed requests, however, you may ask follow-up questions regarding this request. If you have questions or believe we have misinterpreted your request, please feel free to contact the Lynnwood Police Department at (425) 670-5600. title: Performance Evaluation (Laconia Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Laconia Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation(s) for the current city/town manager and administrator. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: NCAA Financial Report (Virginia Commonwealth University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NCAA Financial Report (Virginia Commonwealth University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009 & 2024.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Child care provider data (North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Child care provider data (North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Historical data on all licensed child care providers (active and closed) from as far back as data systems will allow to present. I also request data on license-exempt providers, if available. Specifically, I request the following information as it is available:

1)	Provider name or ID
2)	Street number
3)	Street name
4)	City
5)	Zip code
6)	Licensed capacity for each year (by child age group if available)
7)	Enrollment for each year (by child age group if available)
8)	Provider type (e.g., center-based provider, home-based provider, Head Start, school-based pre-K, etc.) for each year
9)	First license issue (open) date
10)	Most recent license issue date
11)	Most recent license expiration date or close date
12)	Ages served for each year
13)	Hours of operation for each year
14)	QRIS rating for each year (please explain who is required to participate and whether there are financial supports based on rating)
15)	Accepts Child Care and Development Fund subsidies for each year
16)	Accepts other types of subsidies for each year (please list types)
17)	Prices for each year (by child age group if available)
18)	Violation records

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gabrielle Pepin

communications.subject: DCDEE Data Request 273
communications.communication:
Greetings Gabrielle,



This email is to notify you that the Division of Child Development and Early Education (DCDEE) has received your data request. It will be identified moving forward as Data Request 273.



DCDEE strives to provide accurate and timely data.  We appreciate your patience as we work to enable legal, ethical, and actionable data use for you and your colleagues in accordance with the Department's priorities.  The well-being, protection, and security of children, families, the early childhood workforce and DCDEE staff is critically important and includes safeguarding the privacy and security of their personal information.



If this is a recurring request, please allow 5 business days for a response.  If this is a new request, please allow adequate time to ensure adherence to local, state, and federal policies and procedures.  Our goal is to ensure the security of sensitive and personally identifiable data and mitigates the risks of unauthorized disclosure of these data.



Kind regards,


Jasmine Hawthorne
She/Her
DCDEE Data Analyst
Division of Child Development and Early Education, Office of the Director
NC Department of Health and Human Services<https://www.ncdhhs.gov/>

"Good morning,

 

I followed back up with our Regulatory Team to learn more about the timeline for completing this request. We are having some issues with our data warehouse that they along with IT have been working to resolve.

title: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

TO: Freedom of Information Officer

Park District of La Grange

536 East Avenue

La Grange, Illinois 60525

DATE: March 11, 2026

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal

To the Freedom of Information Officer:

This is a request for public records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). I am requesting the following records regarding tree removal and forestry management at Sedgwick Park:

Work Orders and Contracts: All documents, work orders, or contracts with third-party landscaping or arboricultural services regarding the removal of trees at Sedgwick Park from January 1, 2025, to the present.

Internal Assessments: Any arborists' reports, tree health evaluations, or staff memos recommending or justifying the removal of specific trees within Sedgwick Park.

Correspondence: All correspondence (including emails, letters, and internal memos) between Park District staff, Board Commissioners, and/or contractors concerning tree removal, stump grinding, or planned reforestation/replanting at Sedgwick Park.

Financial Records: All invoices or receipts related to the costs incurred for these tree removal services.

Request for Fee Waiver (Public Interest)
Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/6(c), I am requesting a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is in the public interest because:

Public Asset Management: Sedgwick Park is a significant public resource. Information regarding the removal of its natural canopy is of high interest to the community.

Non-Commercial: The records are being sought to inform the public and are not for personal or commercial gain.

Environmental Impact: Understanding the management of the "Urban Forest" within local parks relates directly to the welfare of the residents and the maintenance of public land.

Instructions for Response
Format: Please provide these records in an electronic format to the email address provided below.

Statutory Deadlines: As required by 5 ILCS 140/3(d), if the Park District fails to respond within the five-business-day window (or a valid extension period), the District may not impose fees and may not treat this request as "unduly burdensome."

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption for each redaction and provide the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

Thank you,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange

Disregard information below this line:

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Freedom of Information Act Request – Sedgwick Park Tree Removal / Notice of Extension
communications.communication:
March 12, 2026

VIA EMAIL: 206618-12880172@requests.muckrock.com

Re:        Freedom of Information Act – Extension Letter

To Whom it May Concern:

The Park District of La Grange (“District”) has received your Illinois Freedom of Information Act (“FOIA”) request dated 3/12/26 seeking electronic copies of:

Tree removal and forestry management at Sedgwick Park:
Work Orders and Contracts: All documents, work orders, or contracts with third-party landscaping or arboricultural services regarding the removal of trees at Sedgwick Park from January 1, 2025, to the present.
Internal Assessments: Any arborists' reports, tree health evaluations, or staff memos recommending or justifying the removal of specific trees within Sedgwick Park.
Correspondence: All correspondence (including emails, letters, and internal memos) between Park District staff, Board Commissioners, and/or contractors concerning tree removal, stump grinding, or planned reforestation/replanting at Sedgwick Park.
Financial Records: All invoices or receipts related to the costs incurred for these tree removal services.

The undersigned FOIA officer has prepared this extension letter in response to your FOIA request. The District is extending the time to respond to your FOIA request by five (5) additional business days pursuant to Section 3(e) of FOIA because: the request requires collecting a substantial number of specified records, the requested records require examination and evaluation by personnel having the necessary competence and discretion to determine if the responsive records, or any part thereof, are exempt from disclosure pursuant to applicable FOIA exemptions, and because your request cannot be complied with within 5 business days without unduly burdening District operations. 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(ii); (v); (vi).

The District will respond to your FOIA request on or before Thursday, March 26, 2026.

Sincerely,

Linda Muth
FOIA Officer
Park District of La Grange
536 East Avenue
La Grange, IL 60525
(708) 352-1762 Phone

communications.communication:
March 25, 2026

VIA EMAIL: (206618-12880172@requests.muckrock.com)

Re:        Freedom of Information Act – Response

To Whom it May Concern:

The Park District of La Grange is responding to your FOIA request dated 3/12/26 for which we previously issued a notice of extension letter extending the required response date to 3/26/26.  Your Illinois Freedom of Information Act (“FOIA”) requested electronic copies of the following records: 

Tree removal and forestry management at Sedgwick Park:
Work Orders and Contracts: All documents, work orders, or contracts with third-party landscaping or arboricultural services regarding the removal of trees at Sedgwick Park from January 1, 2025, to the present.
Internal Assessments: Any arborists' reports, tree health evaluations, or staff memos recommending or justifying the removal of specific trees within Sedgwick Park.
Correspondence: All correspondence (including emails, letters, and internal memos) between Park District staff, Board Commissioners, and/or contractors concerning tree removal, stump grinding, or planned reforestation/replanting at Sedgwick Park.
Financial Records: All invoices or receipts related to the costs incurred for these tree removal services.

The Park District has conducted a diligent search for records related to your request.  Per your request, we have uploaded the responsive documents to: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2239013
We have provided 3 zip files which include zip file 1 containing 49 PDF documents, zip file 2 containing 31 PDF documents, and zip file 3 (PST format) containing 67 emails.  

Partial Denial:
The requested records contain information exempt from disclosure under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(b), which protects private information, including personal email addresses, personal phone numbers, and home addresses as defined in 5 ILCS 140/2(c-5). Additionally, disclosure of this information would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c). Accordingly, this information has been redacted.   
    
We have not provided records that are publicly available on our website in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, 5 ILCS 140/8.5. The subject of your request has been a highly discussed topic and is included in memos, reports, meeting minutes, and board meeting packets as far back as December 2022, which can be accessed at:
https://pdlg.org/about-us/committee-meetings-agendas-and-minutes

The person responsible for the partial denial of your request is the FOIA Officer named below. You have a right to have this decision reviewed by the Public Access Counselor at the Office of the Attorney General, 500 South 2nd Street, Springfield, Illinois 62701, 877-299-3642, public.access@ilag.gov. You also have the right to seek judicial review in the Circuit Court in accordance with 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a request for review with the Public Access Counselor, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this letter. Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this letter when filing a request for review with the Public Access Counselor.

Sincerely,

Linda Muth
FOIA Officer
Park District of La Grange
536 East Avenue
La Grange, IL 60525
(708) 352-1762 Phone

Option 2: We download the records onto a thumb drive and send it through the mail directly to your office. Mykayla Moody 😊

title: Request on Political Appointees (U.S. Trade Representative)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (U.S. Trade Representative)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (U.S. Trade Representative)

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (U.S. Trade Representative)
communications.communication:
Ms. Wang,

The Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR) did not receive your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated July 7, 2025 until today. We have assigned it tracking number FY25-133. Please cite this number in any future communications with our office regarding your request.

Our office will provide you with a determination on or before August 29, 2025.

Thank you,
Monique


Monique T. Ricker
FOIA Program Manager/Attorney

EXECUTIVE OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT
OFFICE OF THE UNITED STATES TRADE REPRESENTATIVE
WASHINGTON DC 20508

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (U.S. Trade Representative)
communications.communication:
Ms. Wang,

The search for and/or review of records responsive to Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request no. FY25-133 is underway. Because your request requires USTR to search for and collect the requested records from separate offices or facilities we will require at least an additional ten working days to process due to “unusual circumstances.” Therefore, we would like to provide you with the opportunity to limit the scope of your request.

If you have any questions or wish to discuss reformulation or an alternative time frame for your search, please contact the FOIA Office at FOIA@ustr.eop.gov. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #FY25-133
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on July 8, 2025. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Aug. 29, 2025. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date.  You had assigned it reference number #FY25-133.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #FY25-133
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Wang,

As indicated in our August 28 email, the search for and review of records responsive to your requests are underway, but your requests require additional time to process due to unusual circumstances.  The time it takes to respond to each request varies depending on the complexity of the request and the number of other requests which are already pending. In certain instances, the FOIA request requires us to collect records from separate offices or facilities, which may add to the time needed to process the request.   We will keep you posted as records become available.

If you have any additional questions or would like to limit the scope of your request, please contact the FOIA Office at FOIA@ustr.eop.gov.

communications.subject: FOIA Request FY25-133 Partial Release
communications.communication:
This email is a partial response from the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR) to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request FY25-133 submitted on July 7, 2025 requesting:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.
2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

The timeframe for the search was between January 20, 2025 and March 5, 2026.

We have located 34 responsive pages which we are releasing in part. Portions of the documents have been redacted because we reasonably foresee that disclosure would harm an interest protected by FOIA Exemption 6, which protects personal information, the release of which would not shed light on the performance of the agency's statutory duties.

We will continue to provide records as they become available. If you have any questions or would like help refining your search, please contact the FOIA Office at FOIA@ustr.eop.gov or (202) 395-0418.

files.title: FY25-133 Partial Release

communications.subject: FY25-133 Final Response [ thread::23y5a6dBekGOQ2CCR7YR4A8:: ]
communications.communication:
This email is the final response of the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR) to Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) No. FY25-133 submitted on August 4, 2025 requesting resumes for all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request The timeframe for the search was between January 20, 2025 and March 20, 2026.

After a search of our files, we have located 12 additional responsive pages which we are releasing in part. Portions of the documents have been redacted because we reasonably foresee that disclosure would harm an interest protected by FOIA Exemption 6, which protects personal information the release of which would not shed light on the performance of the agency's statutory duties.

This constitutes a complete response to your request. You may contact the FOIA Office at FOIA@ustr.eop.gov or 202 395-0418 for any further assistance and to discuss any aspect of your request. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.

If you are not satisfied with the response to this request, you may also administratively appeal by writing to: FOIA Office, GSD/RDF; ATTN: Arthur Tsao, Anacostia Naval Annex, Bldg. 410/Door 123, 250 Murray Lane, S.W., Washington, D.C. 20509.

Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of the response to your request. Both the letter and the envelope should be clearly marked: “Freedom of Information Act Appeal" and should include a reference to the FOIA Case File number listed above. Heightened security in force may delay mail delivery; therefore, we suggest that you also email any such appeal to foia@ustr.eop.gov.

In the event you are dissatisfied with the results of any such appeal, judicial review will thereafter be available to you in the United States District Court for the judicial district in which you reside or have your principal place of business, or in the District of Columbia, where we searched for the records you requested.

title: DOD banned books

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DOD banned books
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All communications, reports, memos, guidance documents and any other records about the 595 books removed from Defense Department schools and libraries. For reference, a full list of the titles can be found in the linked memorandum order: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iKxUEllBpsap4cmH_vfWtzv0h069jkSc/view. 

I additionally request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.communication:
Dear Ileana Garnand:

This letter is to acknowledge receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated October 02, 2025, which I received November 13, 2025, for a copy of all communications, reports, memos, guidance documents and any other records about the 595 books removed from Defense Department schools and libraries. For reference, a full list of the titles can be found in the linked memorandum order: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iKxUEllBpsap4cmH_vfWtzv0h069jkSc/view. I additionally request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Ileana Garnand Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2100406/".

Your request has been assigned FOIA case number 26-F-00001.

Under the administrative rules of the FOIA program, the agency has 20 working days from the date of this letter to respond to your request.  It is important to note your request will be processed in the order it was received.  Also, you are a considered a Private Individual requester, and as such, is entitled to 2 hours of search time to find request documents and 100 pages of copying free of charge. If charges exceed the amount stated, you will be contacted for approval before proceeding with the FOIA process.

Your request will be answered as quickly as possible.  If you have any questions, please contact our office at FOIA@dodea.edu.






Ms. Rachel Swann

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Virginia Commonwealth University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Virginia Commonwealth University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Henderson County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Henderson County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police staff roster (May 2021) (Bellevue Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Phil Mocek, 
 
Thank you for submitting your request for public records to the City of Bellevue Police Department. Your request was received in our office on 5/27/2021 8:34:06 PM and given the reference number P001706-052721 for tracking purposes. The details of your request are below.  
 
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

Upload documents directly: https://https://www.muckrock.comhttps://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fbellevue-police-department-816%252Fpolice-staff-roster-may-2021-bellevue-police-department-112919%252F%253Femail%253DBPDrecords%252540bellevuewa.gov&url_auth_token=AAAkAgAcAIBAgMaT31DfQyYqpX4%3A1lmTEO%3AMGI286SVcIFReGXAexd18lZRV94 
The City must provide a preliminary response to your request within five (5) business days (RCW 42.56.520); the day the request is received does not count as one of the five (5) days. Weekends and holidays observed by the City are also excluded in the calculation. For additional information regarding our response options, please click here: Prompt Response Required. The City of Bellevue has adopted a  Public Records Fee Schedule, please review it for information associated with fees that may be assessed pursuant to your request. 
You may monitor the progress of your request via the link below; you will receive an email when your request has been completed. We thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
City of Bellevue Police Department

Phil Mocek,

Your request is now closed; we have nothing additionally responsive to this request. 

Pursuant to the Public Records Act RCW 42.56, responsive records may be redacted or exempt. Please review the accompanying privilege log for further information, if such redactions or exemptions have been applied. 
Per the State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and City Public Records Act Rules (Section 6), a public records request may be considered abandoned and subsequently closed if a requestor fails to fulfill their obligation to claim an installment of records; claim the entire production of records; inspect the records; or fails to pay the deposit or final payment for requested records within 30 days of this notification. 
If you believe the information furnished has been incorrectly redacted or is incomplete, you may file a written appeal. The appeal must include your name and address, a copy of the redacted document and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. Bellevue Police Department RPC 4.4 Inadvertent Disclosure Notification: CR 26 (b) (6) Claw Back Provision *** Please read this notice carefully *** 
Dear legal practitioner: Please take notice that we take reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of privileged or confidential materials, including those protected by the attorney-client privilege and/or work product doctrine, when responding to request for records.  These precautions include, but are not limited to, carefully locating and assembling responsive records, performing an intra-departmental review of the records for purposes of determining whether they contain any privileged or confidential materials, forwarding the records to the County Prosecuting Attorney's Office for independent legal review, meeting and conferring with the Prosecuting Attorney's Office to compare and reconcile any conflicting results of our separate independent reviews and, if necessary, creating a withholding log.  Despite our having taken these precautions, occasionally a mistake is made and privileged or confidential documents are inadvertently produced.  To that end, we wanted to notify you that there is a possibility that the records being produced may contain privileged or confidential materials.  The inadvertent or unintentional production of privileged or confidential documents, without a privileged or confidentiality designation should not be considered a waiver in whole or in part of any claim of privileged or confidential treatment, and any document that initially is produced without bearing a privileged or confidentiality designation may later be so designated.  In the event that you believe you have received privileged or confidential materials, we would ask that you promptly notify us of this fact and take action as necessary to comply with the obligations set forth in Rule of Professional Conduct 4.4 and/or Civil Rule 26 (b)(6).

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam:

I appeal.   In the record you provided in response to my request, you redacted the names, identification numbers, and department/division/unit of approximately four of your employees. As justification for those redactions, you cited RCW 42.56.240(1), which states in its entirety, "Specific intelligence information and specific investigative records compiled by investigative, law enforcement, and penology agencies, and state agencies vested with the responsibility to discipline members of any profession, the nondisclosure of which is essential to effective law enforcement or for the protection of any person's right to privacy;").

Public employees' names, identification numbers, and department/division/unit, are neither specific intelligence information nor specific investigative records.

Further, it appears that you did not provide this tabular data in any of the formats I requested, which were OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. You provided a word processing document, formatted for printing.  This document is not suitable for searching by machine or by combining into other tabular formats. Imagine if every spreadsheet you needed to work with was instead laid out in a "Word document." You could not review it in standard spreadsheet software, import it into a database, or perform any of a variety of other data searching and manipulation tasks on it. The result is less useful to the public.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam:

In consideration of my appeal, sent July 14, 2021, of your processing of my May 27, 2021, request for your staff roster, and of future requests for records that contain names, identification numbers, or department/division/unit of public employees, please see Municipal Research and Services Center's February 24, 2020, guidance, "New Ruling Provides Guidance on `Specific Intelligence'": <https://mrsc.org/Home/Stay-Informed/MRSC-Insight/February-2020/New-Ruling-on-Specific-Intelligence.aspx>.  In it, author Steve Goss references West v. City of Tacoma, No. 51487-7-II (Published January 28, 2020), Division II of the Court of Appeals.

Reading that opinion, I find the following (pp25-26) to be of particular relevance to your processing of my request:

==== BEGIN QUOTED MATERIAL ================
Under RCW 42.56.240(1), specific intelligence information and specific investigative records may be exempt from production. This exemption is intended to protect the integrity of law enforcement investigations. Koenig v. Thurston County, 175 Wn.2d 837, 843, 287 P.3d 523 (2012). To qualify for this exemption, the record must be (1) specific information that is intelligence or investigative in nature; (2) compiled by an investigative, law enforcement, or penological agency; and (3) essential to law enforcement or the protection of privacy. RCW 42.56.240(1); Wade’s Eastside Gun Shop, Inc. v. Dep’t of Labor and Indus., 185 Wn.2d 270, 281, 372 P.3d 97 (2016).

Specific investigative records are records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party. Koenig, 175 Wn.2d at 843. A specific investigation is “designed to ferret out criminal activity or to shed light on some other allegation of malfeasance.” Columbian Publ’g Co. v. City of Vancouver, 36 Wn. App. 25, 31, 671 P.2d 280 (1983). The City does not argue that the redacted documents were investigative records, and because the redacted records did not result from a particular investigation, these records cannot be specific investigative records. Thus, our first focus is on the question of whether the information is “specific intelligence information.”

“Specific intelligence information” is not defined in the PRA. “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” Haines-Marchel v. Dep’t of Corr., 183 Wn. App. 655, 669, 334 P.3d 99 (2014).

In King County v. Sheehan, we held that a list of police officers’ names was not specific intelligence information. 114 Wn. App. 325, 337-38, 57 P.3d 307 (2002). Because the PRA does not define the term “specific intelligence information,” we looked to a dictionary definition to determine its ordinary meaning. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337. We defined intelligence in this context as “‘the gathering or distribution of information, especially secret information,’ or ‘information about an enemy’ or ‘the evaluated conclusions drawn from such information.’” King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337 (quoting RANDOM HOUSE UNABRIDGED DICTIONARY 990 (2d ed. 1993)). Further, we held that the exemption applied to specific intelligence information, which suggested an even narrower interpretation of the exemption. King County, 114 Wn. App.  at 337.
==== END QUOTED MATERIAL ================

The material you redacted is not records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party.  The "specific intelligence information" exemption described in RCW 42.56.240(1) does not apply.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Appeal of Public Records Request No. P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Mocek,

Your appeal of the City of Bellevue's response to public records request number P001706-052721 was forwarded to me yesterday, July 22, 2021. I have carefully considered the appeal and determined that the redactions made were appropriate. As clarified in the attached document, no employee names or badge numbers were redacted. Lines without names and/or numbers reflect that those positions are currently vacant. The names of units with undercover officers were redacted under RCW 42.56.240(1) because the nondisclosure of this information is essential to protect the integrity of law enforcement activities and to maintain officer safety as it may reveal the identity of individuals who are or were performing an undercover or covert law enforcement activity.

The original format for the staff roster you requested is Microsoft Word. This information does not currently exist in another format but, as a courtesy, the City can create an Excel document with the information. If that is agreeable, we will send you this information again in Excel format.

Thank you,

Kathleen

Kathleen Kline
Assistant City Attorney
City of Bellevue
450 110th Avenue NE
P.O. Box 90012
Bellevue, WA 98009
Phone: 425-452-4085
kkline@bellevuewa.gov<mailto:kkline@bellevuewa.gov>

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Kline:

I am surprised to learn that your agency's staff roster is maintained as a word processor file rather than in a database of some sort.  If that is truly the case, then you are under no obligation to transform it for me.  If, however, it is in a database and the word processor file is generated from data in that database, then I ask that you export to a spreadsheet file instead of to a word processor file.

Names of units, with or without undercover officers, are not records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party.  42.56.240(1), the "specific intelligence information" exemption, does not apply, regardless of whether or not nondisclosure of that information is essential to protect the integrity of law enforcement activities or to maintain officer safety.

Please see Municipal Research and Services Center's February 24, 2020, guidance, "New Ruling Provides Guidance on `Specific Intelligence'": <https://mrsc.org/Home/Stay-Informed/MRSC-Insight/February-2020/New-Ruling-on-Specific-Intelligence.aspx>. In it, author Steve Goss references West v. City of Tacoma, No. 51487-7-II (Published January 28, 2020), Division II of the Court of Appeals.

Reading that opinion, I find the following (pp25-26) to be of particular relevance to your processing of my request:

==== BEGIN QUOTED MATERIAL ================
Under RCW 42.56.240(1), specific intelligence information and specific investigative records may be exempt from production. This exemption is intended to protect the integrity of law enforcement investigations. Koenig v. Thurston County, 175 Wn.2d 837, 843, 287 P.3d 523 (2012). To qualify for this exemption, the record must be (1) specific information that is intelligence or investigative in nature; (2) compiled by an investigative, law enforcement, or penological agency; and (3) essential to law enforcement or the protection of privacy. RCW 42.56.240(1); Wade’s Eastside Gun Shop, Inc. v. Dep’t of Labor and Indus., 185 Wn.2d 270, 281, 372 P.3d 97 (2016).

Specific investigative records are records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party. Koenig, 175 Wn.2d at 843. A specific investigation is “designed to ferret out criminal activity or to shed light on some other allegation of malfeasance.” Columbian Publ’g Co. v. City of Vancouver, 36 Wn. App. 25, 31, 671 P.2d 280 (1983). The City does not argue that the redacted documents were investigative records, and because the redacted records did not result from a particular investigation, these records cannot be specific investigative records. Thus, our first focus is on the question of whether the information is “specific intelligence information.”

“Specific intelligence information” is not defined in the PRA. “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” Haines-Marchel v. Dep’t of Corr., 183 Wn. App. 655, 669, 334 P.3d 99 (2014).

In King County v. Sheehan, we held that a list of police officers’ names was not specific intelligence information. 114 Wn. App. 325, 337-38, 57 P.3d 307 (2002). Because the PRA does not define the term “specific intelligence information,” we looked to a dictionary definition to determine its ordinary meaning. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337. We defined intelligence in this context as “‘the gathering or distribution of information, especially secret information,’ or ‘information about an enemy’ or ‘the evaluated conclusions drawn from such information.’” King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337 (quoting RANDOM HOUSE UNABRIDGED DICTIONARY 990 (2d ed. 1993)). Further, we held that the exemption applied to specific intelligence information, which suggested an even narrower interpretation of the exemption. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337.
==== END QUOTED MATERIAL ================

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Mocek,

Thank you for your email. Again, the existing police department staff roster (employee list), that would show all the information you requested (first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers) is only maintained as a word document and was not generated from a database. As a courtesy, we have compiled the datasets you requested by creating a new record from multiple sources. The attached excel and csv documents have the (1) first name, (2) last name, (3) title, (4) badge number, and (5) area of assignment for police department staff.

RCW 42.56.240(1) exempts both “intelligence information” and “investigative records” compiled by a law enforcement agency if the information is “essential to effective law enforcement” or needed to protect a person’s privacy rights.” Intelligence information includes records that may reveal particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information, the disclosure of which would harm future law enforcement efforts. See Haines-Marchel v. State, Dept. of Corrections, 183 Wn. App. 655, 334 P.3d 99 (2014) (concluding identity of informants and criteria used to evaluate the reliability of informants was specific intelligence information); Gronquist v. State, Department of Corrections, 177 Wn. App. 389, 313 P.3d 416 (2013), review denied, 180 Wn.2d 1004, 321 P.3d 1207 (2014) (concluding prison surveillance recordings was intelligence information because they would reveal information about investigative methods). As the quote you excerpt in your email from the recent West case makes clear, “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” West v. City of Tacoma, 12 Wn. App. 2d 45, 71, 334 P.3d 99 (2020), quoting Haines-Marchel, 183 Wn. App. at 669.

Kathleen

Kathleen Kline
Assistant City Attorney
City of Bellevue
450 110th Avenue NE
P.O. Box 90012
Bellevue, WA 98009
Phone: 425-452-4085
kkline@bellevuewa.gov<mailto:kkline@bellevuewa.gov>
Pronouns: she/her/hers

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P001629-031226
communications.communication:
Dear Phil Mocek, 
 
Thank you for submitting your request for public records to the City of Bellevue Police Department. Your request was received in our office on 3/12/2026 2:27:17 PM and given the reference number P001629-031226 for tracking purposes. The details of your request are below.  
 
Bellevue Police Department 
Washington Public Records Act Office 
P.O. Box 90012 
Bellevue, WA 98004 

March 12, 2026 

To Whom It May Concern: 
## PRESERVATION DEMAND ## 
Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt. 
Please immediately: 

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval. 

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution. 
## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##. 

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 

## FEE REQUEST ## 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 
1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 
2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request: 

Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles 
(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered 
but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005) 
i. 44372D — Command vehicle (2005) 
j. 54148D — Riot fencing trailer (2011) 
k. 54362D — CSI van with generator (2012) 
l. 50295D — SWAT cutaway van (2008) 
m. 54117D — CSI van (2011) 
n. 24208D — Weapons vehicle (1987) 

Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern): 
o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP" 
p. All plates with the prefix "XMT" 

Other: 
a. BCH-478 
b. 826-ZVA 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek 

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: 
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238469/ 
Filed via MuckRock.com 
E-mail (Preferred): 206500-00859330@requests.muckrock.com 
PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS 
For mailed responses, please address (see note): 
MuckRock News 
DEPT MR 206500 
263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 
PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.communication:
Dear Phil Mocek, 
 
Thank you for submitting your request for public records to the City of Bellevue Police Department. Your request was received in our office on 3/19/2026 5:35:20 PM and given the reference number P001796-031926 for tracking purposes. The details of your request are below.  
 
Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data. communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line. Anything below will not be viewable to staff. ---

"03/19/2026
P001796-031926
 
Phil Mocek,
 
 The Bellevue Police Department has received your public records request for the following:
N/A / Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data. (Unrecognized email flagged by CCO)
 
Please be advised, your request was sent to an email address that cannot accept responses and was flagged by the City Clerk's Office. In future, please create requests from your account or send an email to BPDRecords@bellevuewa.gov. 
 
In your request, you mentioned three other requests. Please be advised, we do not have a request with the reference number P001706-052721. Considering it would have been created in 2021 it is most likely past retention and no longer accessible because of its age. P001629-031226 was closed as a duplicate request to P001641-031226, which is an open request for different records. Please also be advised that we are not permitted to access websites or cloud storage to provide records, but we can provide them via our portal and you will be able to download them and place them into whichever system you prefer.
 
Your request is still considered open. You should anticipate an additional response by the end of business on 3/27/2026, if not sooner. Please be advised, our staff roster exists and is not a spreadsheet. 
 
If you have any questions regarding your disclosure request or any other matter, please contact the Bellevue Police Department Records Unit via the Public Records Portal or by telephone at (425) 452-6917. 
 
Thank you,
BPD Disclosure
Bellevue Police Department"

communications.subject: [Records Center] Police Records Request :: P001796-031926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line. Anything below will not be viewable to staff. --- 
03/24/2026 P001796-031926 Phil Mocek, 
Records responsive to your request are now available via the City of Bellevue Public Records Portal website. Pop-ups may need to be enabled on your internet browser to access your files for download. This concludes our search of records in the custody of the Bellevue Police Department. This is our final, definitive response and your request is closed.  As the city does not intend to further address the request, the Public Records Act one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has now started to run. Pursuant to the Public Records Act RCW 42.56, responsive records may be redacted or exempt. Please review the accompanying privilege log for further information, if such redactions or exemptions have been applied. Per the State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and City Public Records Act Rules (Section 6), a public records request may be considered abandoned and subsequently closed if a requestor fails to fulfill their obligation to claim an installment of records; claim the entire production of records; inspect the records; or fails to pay the deposit or final payment for requested records within 30 days of this notification. Please be advised, you may file a written appeal if you believe information has been incorrectly redacted or records have been incorrectly withheld. The appeal must include your name and address, a copy of the redacted document and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. Please mail your appeal to: Bellevue Police Department 
Attn: Records Supervisor 
P.O. Box 90012 
Bellevue, WA  98009-9012 If you require any further assistance from the Bellevue Police Department regarding this matter or have questions, please contact the Records Unit via the Public Records Portal or by telephone at (425) 452-6917 within 30 days of this notification. Thank you, Jessica Havens 
Bellevue Police Department 
RPC 4.4 Inadvertent Disclosure Notification: CR 26 (b) (6) Claw Back Provision *** Please read this notice carefully *** Dear legal practitioner: Please take notice that we take reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of privileged or confidential materials, including those protected by the attorney-client privilege and/or work product doctrine, when responding to request for records.  These precautions include, but are not limited to, carefully locating and assembling responsive records, performing an intra-departmental review of the records for purposes of determining whether they contain any privileged or confidential materials, forwarding the records to the County Prosecuting Attorney's Office for independent legal review, meeting and conferring with the Prosecuting Attorney's Office to compare and reconcile any conflicting results of our separate independent reviews and, if necessary, creating a withholding log.  Despite our having taken these precautions, occasionally a mistake is made and privileged or confidential documents are inadvertently produced.  To that end, we wanted to notify you that there is a possibility that the records being produced may contain privileged or confidential materials.  The inadvertent or unintentional production of privileged or confidential documents, without a privileged or confidentiality designation should not be considered a waiver in whole or in part of any claim of privileged or confidential treatment, and any document that initially is produced without bearing a privileged or confidentiality designation may later be so designated.  In the event that you believe you have received privileged or confidential materials, we would ask that you promptly notify us of this fact and take action as necessary to comply with the obligations set forth in Rule of Professional Conduct 4.4 and/or Civil Rule 26 (b)(6). 

 
To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Bellevue Public Records Center (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXbUWSWnJYM-2FRewwjUGmaQvl-2Foebzj8OR-2F9FwJY48-2FiaJ4jQOGvZL4X-2F2Gx-2Bi0NaDrzCGoxj77nEMQGv-2BZSBXn8Y-3Dw6aO_wlzVhu-2F-2FS2zzloS3j1z9ymqKcFaOfBK4aU1EFb6CkHmdlr9NP6HbpRIq1cW38tpigsMChXYrrMauWbIJ09Lvioh6bdq-2Bm-2FtLotP-2BZVCh0UUHcfBZT-2BkkwTbXE8VOhGsRAjfFechS2rTI6Ks1ng2ktT94vLAzmZqvzxmYl7JKNFVYbvy5Ui2PhmDbTSR7XQpu0llVxPtq3pGCuCkk22VL1wDFTkBQntM7jAioEVsQSCnEwfrwVB1f4-2FfdqO55-2FbmqxSaugGs1XG4P88MYYj8Mw709BZd9d7l8HBr-2BNRw2J-2Ff56J9L7-2FVG9ec6jm1vjiVFNfehc8e1Zen6gstm5SjMsUtoAap99gupzd-2B9T9d-2BE0yP0AVamriIgOB8-2FzmC-2F9Xtext55Yye3axIwIJLpe2qJA-3D-3D)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All administrative tort claims, and their attachments, submitted by Ilan Miller to the County of Marin;

Any responses, investigations, by the County of Marin or its insurance carrier about Ilan Miller's tort claim.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

March 24, 2026 Miriam Bhimani Miriam.bhimani@gmail.com  RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019200-030826 Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Request seeking the following information: Supply the Investigative Memoranda with the following File Numbers: 
INV-09577 
INV-09620
