INV-09751 
INV-09754 
INV-09755 
INV-09758 
INV-09760 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. Please visit the following link and enter your username and password to access the records: http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx . Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(b), “Private information, unless disclosure is required by another provision of this Act, a State or federal law or a court order,” and pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), “Personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs the legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. The disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees and officials shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. 
Redactions were additionally made to conceal individual identities pursuant to Section 7(1)(d)(iv) which allows a public to withhold records/information that would unavoidably reveal the identity of a complainant or witness. nformation has additionally been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(a): Information specifically prohibited by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or state law. Specifically, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5(r) in accordance with Illinois Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at its direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored. 105 ILCS 10/(d): Release of a minor’s record may only be authorized by the parent or guardian in accordance with the Illinois Student Records Act (105 ILCS 10/1 et seq.). Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a), this section exempts from disclosure information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law. Specifically, the Abused and Neglected Child Reporting Act, section 325 ILCS 5/1, states that all records concerning reports of child abuse and neglect or records concerning referrals under this Act and all records generated as a result of such reports or referrals, shall be confidential and shall not be disclosed except as specifically authorized by this Act or other applicable law. Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5(v): vulnerability assessments, security measures, and response policies or plans that are designed to identify, prevent, or respond to potential attacks upon a community’s population or systems, facilities or installations, but only to the extent that disclosure could reasonably be expected to expose the vulnerability or jeopardize the effectiveness of the measures, policies, or plans, or the safety of the personnel who implement them or the public. Information exempt under this item may include such things as details pertaining to the mobilization or deployment of personnel or equipment, to the operation of communication systems or protocols, to cybersecurity vulnerabilities, or to tactical operations. Section 7.5(h) of the FOIA, exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). Lastly, Section 7.5(v) of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act exempts from public disclosure the names and information of individuals who have applied for or received Firearm Owner’s Identification (FOID) cards under the Firearm Owners Identification Card Act. 
INV-09711 INV-09748 RESPONSE: The records you seek have been withheld as they are exempt from disclosure pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m) which exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body. INV-09697 INV-09698 INV-09761 INV-09765 RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office conducted a due diligence search and there are no responsive records as no memorandums were created. In case of denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706 . You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.  Thank you for your request. Sincerely, Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation for the current district superintendent at the time of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Please see attached.

Please note that any responsive records containing student educational
record information are exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A:5, IV, which
protects confidential files and individual privacy. Furthermore, such
records are protected by the federal Family Educational Rights and Privacy
Act (FERPA), which prohibits the unauthorized release of personally
identifiable information from a student's education record.

Thank you,
Jeremy



-- 


Jeremy Rathbun

Superintendent of Schools

SAU93 - Monadnock Regional School District

600 Old Homestead Highway, Swanzey, NH 03446

solely for the use of the individual or entity to whom they are addressed.
If you have received this email in error, please notify the system manager.
This message contains confidential information and is intended only for the
individual named. If you are not the named addressee you should not
disseminate, distribute or copy this e-mail. Please notify the sender
immediately by e-mail if you have received this e-mail by mistake and
delete this e-mail from your system. If you are not the intended recipient,
you are notified that disclosing, copying, distributing or taking any
action in reliance on the contents of this information is strictly
prohibited.  The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communications
to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School
District are government records available to the public upon request.
Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.

title: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Auburn Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Auburn Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All system audit logs, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting access to your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data (e.g., Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, Motorola/Vigilant), by users affiliated with any agency other than your agency.

2. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) or agency name associated with each external query of your agency's ALPR system.

3. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the user identifier, badge number, or account name associated with each query of your agency's ALPR system, whether the querying user was internal or external to your agency.

4. All records identifying which external agencies are currently authorized to access, query, or receive data from your agency's ALPR system, including any configuration record, permissions list, access control list, or authorized-agency registry maintained in your ALPR platform.  If this information exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot, faithful reproduction, or export of that display to any available file format is an acceptable and required form of production.

Note on vendor-held records: In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure. Vendor custody does not relieve your agency of its production obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

Note on record creation: I am seeking existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Agencies cannot evade their production obligation by characterizing screenshots of existing system displays as "new records."

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P049101-031526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 15, 2026, Reference # P049101-031526 Dear Phil : Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520 and Soter v. Cowles Pub. Co, this letter acknowledges that the City has received your above referenced public records request on 3/15/2026. Due to the number of requests the City is currently processing, and the time period needed to gather and review responsive records for potential exemptions, redactions and third party notices, the City estimates that it will take up to approximately 30 calendar -days from the date of this letter to respond to your request (either in full, or partially through a first installment of records). Please note the following helpful information as the City processes your records request: 
 
  *  The City responds to its numerous Public Records Requests in the order that they are received 
  *  The City may respond to larger records requests by providing responsive records in installments 
  *  The City's production of records to you may require you to pay fees and costs as allowed by law If your request seeks police-related records: these requests frequently generate large sets of responsive records. The City's practice is to respond by providing requested records in installments. First installments typically include responsive documents such as police reports, CAD incident reports, and photos. Later installments may include police in-car video or body camera footage. You will be provided a time estimate for the next installment as each installment becomes available. Narrowing the scope of your request may result in fewer installments, and therefore reduced overall response time. If you are interested in narrowing the scope of your request in any way, please respond to this message or contact our office at (253) 931-3039. If the City needs additional information to clarify your request, or to modify its estimated response date above, City staff will contact you. To view the full details of  this request please log into the Public Records Center.  Please note that this correspondence is for informational purposes only, and you are not required to respond. Katie Sorum 
Records Clerk 
City Clerk's Office 
253-931-3039

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

communications.subject: FW: [EXT] Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Please see attached in response to your records request. You will see in a few of the reports that we have redacted personal information of our AD such as cell phone number as well as her home address in her contract. This information is exempted from disclosure pursuant to KRS 61.878(1)(a) because the information is of a personal nature and the disclosure thereof would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.
Regards,

Office of Legal Affairs and General Counsel
Northern Kentucky University
5320 Campus Drive
Lucas Administrative Center, Room 812
Phone: (859) 572-5587
openrecords@nku.edu<mailto:openrecords@nku.edu>

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Monadnock Regional School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Please see attached.

Please note that any responsive records containing student educational
record information are exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A:5, IV, which
protects confidential files and individual privacy. Furthermore, such
records are protected by the federal Family Educational Rights and Privacy
Act (FERPA), which prohibits the unauthorized release of personally
identifiable information from a student's education record.

Thank you,
Jeremy




-- 


Jeremy Rathbun

Superintendent of Schools

SAU93 - Monadnock Regional School District

p:

(603)903-6977  m: (603)903-3736

a:

600 Old Homestead Highway, Swanzey, NH 03446

This email and any files transmitted with it are confidential and intended
solely for the use of the individual or entity to whom they are addressed.
If you have received this email in error, please notify the system manager.
This message contains confidential information and is intended only for the
individual named. If you are not the named addressee you should not
disseminate, distribute or copy this e-mail. Please notify the sender
immediately by e-mail if you have received this e-mail by mistake and
delete this e-mail from your system. If you are not the intended recipient,
you are notified that disclosing, copying, distributing or taking any
action in reliance on the contents of this information is strictly
prohibited.  The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communications
to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School
District are government records available to the public upon request.
Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Documents sufficient to show all user accounts for the department’s Flock deployment that were active between 3/29/2023 and the date that this request is processed.

Documents that show who has access to the department’s Flock deployment and the access that is enabled/disabled for all user accounts (current and previously suspended, revoked, terminated, dormant, etc.). The report should include all available details including, but not limited to, the following where available: User ID, First Name, Last Name, Title, Role, Status, Identity Provider, Last login, User Mgmt, Search, Device Mgmt, Hotlist Tool.

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
2. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4. A list of network share settings that include:

4a. Networks that I’m sharing -
The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares its Flock Safety ALPR data, also referred to as "Networks that I’m sharing."
4b. Flock networks shared with me -
The names of agencies and organizations that have shared their cameras with the Agency, also referred to as "Flock networks shared with me."

5. Any Non‑email records covering Flock Safety training presentations, published procedures,  directives, training materials, promotional materials, or legal guidance.



Please provide all responsive records in their original electronic and machine readable format. This includes, but is not limited to, spreadsheet files such as CSV or Excel, slide presentation files such as PowerPoint, and any other original native file formats. Please do not substitute scanned images or PDFs for original files, and please do not direct me to your transparency portal in place of providing the records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Hank

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on a timeline for the remainder of the documents from the Freedom of Information request, copied below, and originally submitted on 12/07/2025. Specifically,  the remaining organization audit from March 29, 2023 to December 14, 2025 and network audit from March 29, 2023 to December 15, 2025 for department’s Flock Safety system. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response, or if further clarification is needed.

Thank you for your help.

Hank

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Documents sufficient to show all user accounts for the department’s Flock deployment that were active between 3/29/2023 and the date that this request is processed.

Documents that show who has access to the department’s Flock deployment and the access that is enabled/disabled for all user accounts (current and previously suspended, revoked, terminated, dormant, etc.). The report should include all available details including, but not limited to, the following where available: User ID, First Name, Last Name, Title, Role, Status, Identity Provider, Last login, User Mgmt, Search, Device Mgmt, Hotlist Tool.

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
2. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4. A list of network share settings that include:

4a. Networks that I’m sharing -
The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares its Flock Safety ALPR data, also referred to as "Networks that I’m sharing."
4b. Flock networks shared with me -
The names of agencies and organizations that have shared their cameras with the Agency, also referred to as "Flock networks shared with me."

5. Any Non‑email records covering Flock Safety training presentations, published procedures, directives, training materials, promotional materials, or legal guidance.

Please provide all responsive records in their original electronic and machine readable format. This includes, but is not limited to, spreadsheet files such as CSV or Excel, slide presentation files such as PowerPoint, and any other original native file formats. Please do not substitute scanned images or PDFs for original files, and please do not direct me to your transparency portal in place of providing the records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Hank

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Randolph County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Randolph County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Records & Public Disclosure Section - Thurston County)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Records & Public Disclosure Section - Thurston County)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, The Sunshine Act 5 U.S.C. § 552b, and Washington Public Records Act Title 14 Chapter 44-14 and related. 

Please provide the records in electronic form, and produce them on a rolling basis as they become available for  Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders.

If a record exists but cannot be produced, please state the reason and provide any non-exempt portions. If the record does not exist or is not held by your agency, please confirm which of these applies.

●A copy of their Public Official surety bond that is required by Washington Title 28A.330.060
● A copy of the blanket surety bond
● A copy of their Errors & Omissions (E&O), a Surety Liability Insurance policy, and the Duty of Care policy if applicable.
● A copy of their General Obligation Bonds if applicable.
● A copy of their general long-term bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Crime Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Risk Management Policy if applicable.
● A copy of the following documents if applicable:
○ ACORD 125
○ ACORD 126
○ ACORD 127
○ ACORD 128
○ The documents should indicate the policy number and the insured amount of the policy.
● Certificate of Liability if applicable.
● Public Officials and/or any other bonds pertaining to proof of liability and policies.
Based on any and all losses of financial responsibility due to negligence or dishonesty.
Any and all based on the contract of terms and conditions.
● A copy of their faithful Performance Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Fidelity Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Dishonesty Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Blanket Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Statutory Bond if applicable.
● A copy of the power of attorney for the surety bond company.
● A copy of the Blanket Bond power of attorney for the surety bond company if applicable.
● A copy of their oath of office.
● A copy of their Anti Bribery and Corruption Statement.
● A copy of their Form NDS-1, Registration Statement Pursuant to the Foreign Agents Registration Act.

I am seeking information concerning their public official surety bond, or the Errors & Omissions (E&O), and or the Duty of Care policy that you are required by the state of Washington to obtain before swearing the oath of office.
If any document listed cannot be provided, please respond specifying which document(s) cannot be provided and the reason for each non-disclosure.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anne Sackville

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P039037-121525
communications.communication:
Dear Anne Sackville: 
Thank you for your interest in Thurston County. This is an automated response to inform you that your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 12/15/2025 7:51:09 PM and assigned the reference number P039037-121525 for tracking purposes. 
Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, The Sunshine Act 5 U.S.C. § 552b, and Washington Public Records Act Title 14 Chapter 44-14 and related. 

Please provide the records in electronic form, and produce them on a rolling basis as they become available for Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders.

title: City of Santa Clarita: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded Via Resident Service Center portion of city website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Santa Clarita: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded Via Resident Service Center portion of city website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the "Resident Service Center" option that is accessible via the city's website.

Santa Clarita's graffiti abatement efforts are discussed online here: https://santaclarita.gov/communityservices/graffiti-task-force/three-prong-approach/

Citizens are encourage to uploaded photographs of graffiti using the "Resident Service Center" which can be found here: https://user.govoutreach.com/santaclarita/faq.php?cmd=shell

Time Frame for request: June 1 2020 to March 1st 2026. 

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Santa Clarita. 

Thank you to the city Santa Clarita for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Virginia Military Institute)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Virginia Military Institute)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Public Records Law (Chapter 119, Florida Statutes), I hereby request the following records related to Okaloosa County Sheriffs Department's use of and access to the Pinellas County Sheriff's Office Face Analysis Comparison & Examination System (FACES) and related surveillance technologies:

1. FACES SYSTEM ACCESS POLICIES AND PROCEDURES:
   - All current written policies governing the department's use of the FACES facial recognition system
   - All memoranda of understanding (MOUs), memoranda of agreement (MOAs), interagency agreements, or contracts with Pinellas County Sheriff's Office or any other entity related to FACES access
   - Training materials provided to personnel authorized to use FACES
   - Standard operating procedures (SOPs) for conducting FACES searches
   - Documents describing civil rights protections, limitations on use, or oversight mechanisms for FACES searches

2. ADMINISTRATIVE LOGS AND USAGE RECORDS:
   - Organization Audit reports from FACES showing searches conducted by department personnel for the period of January 1st, 2026 to the date this request is processed if available
   - Documents sufficient to show:
     * Total number of FACES searches conducted by the department
     * User logs identifying which personnel conducted searches (names may be redacted if required by law, but please include badge numbers or employee IDs)
     * Case numbers or incident reports associated with each search
     * Stated reasons or justifications for each search
     * Any internal audit reports reviewing FACES usage for compliance or misuse

3. INTEGRATION AND RELATED TECHNOLOGIES:
   - All contracts, agreements, purchase orders, invoices, or correspondence with FUSUS/Axon or any entity related to FUSUS system implementation or integration
   - Documents describing any integration between FACES and the FUSUS platform or any other real-time surveillance systems
   - Technical specifications or system architecture documents showing how FACES connects or shares data with other surveillance technologies including but not limited to:
     * FUSUS real-time camera integration platform
     * Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs)
     * Predictive policing systems
     * Body-worn cameras
     * ShotSpotter or other gunshot detection systems
     * Private camera registries
   - Any assessments, reports, or internal communications discussing the integration of FACES with other surveillance technologies
   - Documents describing any real-time crime center operations that utilize FACES

4. PERFORMANCE AND ACCURACY:
   - Any internal assessments, audits, or external reports evaluating the accuracy, effectiveness, or reliability of FACES
   - Documents addressing known error rates, particularly regarding demographic disparities
   - Any false positive reports or case files where FACES identification was determined to be incorrect

5. POLICY UPDATES AND OVERSIGHT:
   - Communications with Pinellas County Sheriff's Office regarding policy updates or requirements implemented in 2024
   - Documents related to any citizen oversight, internal affairs reviews, or complaints regarding FACES usage
   - Any reports provided to elected officials, oversight bodies, or the public regarding FACES usage

================================================================================

IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION REGARDING SCOPE OF REQUEST:

This request does NOT seek:
- Personally identifiable information (PII) of individuals who have been subjects of searches or investigations, including names, addresses, dates of birth, social security numbers, or biometric data of searched individuals
- Photographs, facial recognition images, or biometric templates of any individuals in the FACES database
- Specific investigative techniques, methodologies, or operational tactics that would compromise ongoing or future investigations
- Information that would reveal the identity of confidential informants or undercover officers

This request DOES seek:
- Policies, procedures, and governance documents regarding system use
- Aggregate statistical data and anonymized audit logs showing usage patterns, search volume, and compliance
- System integration information at an architectural level (what systems connect to what) without revealing specific security vulnerabilities
- Public accountability records such as oversight reports, accuracy assessments, and policy compliance reviews

I am seeking information about the governance, oversight, financial arrangements, and operational policies surrounding these technologies—not information about specific individuals, active investigations, or sensitive law enforcement techniques. All personal identifying information of subjects, victims, witnesses, or confidential sources should be redacted as required by law.

If any responsive records contain both exempt and non-exempt information, I request that you provide the non-exempt portions with appropriate redactions, as required by Florida Statutes § 119.07(1)(d).

================================================================================

FORMAT AND FEE WAIVER REQUEST:

Please provide the requested records in their original electronic, machine-readable format. For audit reports and logs, please provide data in .csv, .xlsx, or another database format if available. For policy documents, PDF format is acceptable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency and accountability regarding the use of facial recognition technology and surveillance systems. Therefore, I request a waiver of all fees associated with this request. If any fees apply, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

================================================================================

TIME FRAME:

I prefer to receive records electronically by email attachment. Florida law requires a response to this request within a reasonable time, the timeline for this request is 10 business days. If you anticipate difficulty meeting this timeline, please contact me.

If any portion of this request is denied, please:
1. Cite the specific statutory exemption being claimed for each withheld record
2. Provide a detailed explanation of why the exemption applies to the specific information being withheld
3. Indicate whether a redacted version of the record can be provided

Thank you for your assistance. Please contact me if you have any questions about this request or require clarification on any aspect of what is being sought.

Sincerely,

Kyle Searfoss

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am writing to appeal the partial denial of my public records request regarding technology/system documentation. I respectfully contend that the information I am requesting does not fall within the scope of the exemptions cited in your response. Additionally, I am deeply disappointed in the fact that your department did not disclose that certain sections would be redacted/exempt prior to charging me a processing fee. 

Your response withheld Sections 2 and 3 entirely under F.S. 119.071(2)(c)(1) and F.S. 119.071(2)(d). However, the nature of the information I am requesting does not meet the criteria for these exemptions.

F.S. 119.071(2)(c)(1) - Criminal Investigative Information:

This exemption protects "active criminal intelligence information and active criminal investigative information." The records I requested are:
- Procurement and administrative documents showing which systems integrate with FACES to include systems like FUSUS

These are administrative and procurement records, not investigative records related to specific criminal cases or intelligence operations. The information exists independently of any particular investigation and pertains to how the department has configured its technology infrastructure, not how that technology is used in specific investigations.

Example: Knowing that FUSUS integrates with body-worn cameras, ALPR systems, FACES, or CAD systems does not reveal any information about active criminal investigations. This is administrative information about technology procurement and configuration.

F.S. 119.071(2)(d) - Surveillance Techniques or Procedures:

This exemption protects information "revealing surveillance techniques or procedures." The records I requested are:
- What systems connect to FACES and/or FUSUS(not how they are used operationally)
- Hardware specifications and equipment lists (not deployment strategies)

Hardware Redactions in Pileum Contract:

The unsigned Pileum(FUSUS) contract contains redactions over hardware specifications. Hardware information does not fall within either cited exemption:
- Hardware models and equipment specifications are not "criminal investigative information", they are procurement information.
- Equipment lists do not reveal "surveillance techniques", they identify what was purchased not how it is deployed.

Additional Matters:

Unsigned Contract: The Pileum(FUSUS) contract provided appears to be unsigned. Please clarify whether an executed agreement exists and provide the signed version.

Duplicate MOU: The Pinellas County MOU appears to have been provided twice under different names. Please clarify if these are identical documents.

Conclusion:

The information I am requesting is administrative in nature and does not meet the statutory definitions of the exemptions you have cited. I am not seeking:
- Details of active criminal investigations
- Operational procedures or tactical deployments
- Security vulnerabilities or system access credentials
- Specific surveillance methodologies

I request that you:
1. Reconsider the applicability of the cited exemptions to the requested administrative and procurement information.
2. Provide the requested records, or at minimum, provide all segregable portions.
3. Provide unredacted hardware specifications from the FUSUS contract.
4. Provide specific, detailed justification if you maintain these exemptions apply.

Thank you for your reconsideration of this matter.

Sincerely,

Kyle Searfoss

communications.subject: Fw: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
As General Counsel of the Okaloosa County Sheriff's Office, your email dated March 12, 2026 was forwarded to me for further review as to the applicability of Sec. 119.071 (2)(d), Florida Statutes, and Sec. 119.071(2)(c)(1), Florida Statutes, as the basis for the redaction of the records you requested pursuant to a public records request. Upon review of your requests as well as the records, the determination that the records are confidential and exempt pursuant to the two cited statutory provisions was correct. As the records are confidential and exempt from public disclosure, they will not be released pursuant to a public records request.


Marsha Weaver,
General Counsel
Okaloosa County Sheriff's Office
50 2nd Street
Shalimar, FL 32579
